browse.description: Runs direct browser workflows for navigation, inspection, and evidence capture using the repo-owned browser adapter.

benchmark.description: Measures live page performance, compares against baselines, and reports regressions in timing, bundle size, and resource behavior.

canary.description: Monitors a live deployment after release, checks for page failures and regressions, and records evidence-based health reports.

careful.description: Activates destructive-action guardrails for the session, warning before risky commands and operations in shared or production-like environments.

freeze.description: Restricts edits to a specific directory boundary for the session so unrelated files are not accidentally modified.

guard.description: Activates full safety mode by combining destructive-command warnings with a directory-scoped edit boundary.

unfreeze.description: Clears the current edit boundary so file edits are allowed anywhere again.

setup-browser-cookies.description: Imports cookies from a real browser into the configured browser adapter session for authenticated browsing and QA.

setup-deploy.description: Detects and records deploy platform, URL, and verification settings so land-and-deploy can run with less guesswork.

investigate.description: Investigates bugs with a root-cause-first debugging workflow before making fixes.

document-release.description: Audits and updates project documentation after shipped changes so docs, changelog, and release metadata do not drift.

office-hours.description: Runs a product or builder brainstorming session that sharpens the problem, tests premises, compares approaches, and ends in an approved design doc instead of code.

plan-ceo-review.description: Reviews a plan in founder-mode, challenges the problem framing, and lets the user explicitly choose whether to expand, hold, or reduce scope.

plan-design-review.description: Reviews a plan's UI and UX before implementation, rates design completeness, and fills missing design decisions directly into the plan.

design-review.description: Audits a live UI for visual and interaction quality, fixes issues in small verified steps, and reports before/after design score movement.

autoplan.description: Runs the full plan review gauntlet by orchestrating CEO, design, and engineering reviews, auto-deciding mechanical choices and surfacing taste decisions at a final gate.

land-and-deploy.description: Merges a PR or branch, waits for deployment, and verifies the live result with explicit post-merge reporting.

plan-eng-review.description: Reviews an implementation plan for architecture, edge cases, test coverage, and performance before coding starts.

ship.description: Runs a structured ship workflow: assess branch state, verify readiness, prepare a gitmoji commit, and create a PR or shipping-ready output.

learn.description: Manages project learnings by showing, searching, pruning, exporting, and recording durable project insights.

design-shotgun.description: Explores multiple distinct design directions, compares them, captures feedback, and converges on an approved visual direction.

design-consultation.description: Proposes a coherent product design system, explains the rationale, previews the direction, and writes or updates DESIGN.md when approved.

connect-chrome.description: Validates the configured browser runtime, checks whether visible-browser control is available, and explains how to continue with browser-driven workflows.

qa.description: Runs browser-oriented QA workflows, triages issues, and fixes app bugs when the required QA adapter is configured.

qa-only.description: Runs browser-oriented QA in report-only mode without making code changes.

review.description: Reviews current work for bugs, regressions, and missing tests.
